codeReview.summary: Pass. The reviewed snapshot satisfies the baseline-validator cleanup requirements: company lifecycle mutations are board-only and company-scoped, the instructions-bundle write path still normalizes and persists the expected adapterConfig bundle keys, and manual-unblock/issue-detail responses explicitly serialize cleared execution ownership fields as null.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill does not describe a validation-only completion path for stabilization features whose required fixes are already present at HEAD. This worker had to deviate from the documented write-tests-first/implement procedure for a justified no-op validation handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:34-45 requires writing tests first and implementing the feature, while the handoff records `followedProcedure: false` and explains that the worker only audited HEAD and ran validators because the fixes were already landed (/Users/aischool/.factory/missions/082135e3-5840-4875-806d-1c1e9e82e66a/handoffs/2026-04-08T02-06-07-288Z__stabilize-runtime-lifecycle-baseline-validators__41c39ea5-11a7-4e57-a114-c52ba08556d3.json:58-69).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission guidance disagrees on the preferred full-suite test command: mission AGENTS tells workers to use `pnpm test:run -- --maxWorkers=5`, but `.factory/services.yaml` advertises `pnpm test:run -- --maxWorkers=1`. This mismatch can cause unnecessary uncertainty during validation-heavy cleanup tasks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/aischool/.factory/missions/082135e3-5840-4875-806d-1c1e9e82e66a/AGENTS.md:53 specifies `pnpm test:run -- --maxWorkers=5`, while /Users/aischool/work/papierklammer_droid/.factory/services.yaml:5 defines `test: pnpm test:run -- --maxWorkers=1`.

summary: Reviewed the validation-only handoff against the feature metadata, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and the e0789079 snapshot. I found no code-level blockers or regressions for the baseline-validator goals; the only follow-up items are shared-state/documentation gaps around backend-worker no-op validation runs and inconsistent test-command guidance.
